name: Mediona Castle and Les Deus Loop from Sant Joan de Mediona
name_original: Castillo de Mediona y Les Deus — circular desde San Joan de Mediona
description: This difficult 9.0-mile (14.5 km) jogging route from San Joan de Mediona explores Castillo de Mediona and the natural springs of Les Deus.
region_intro: Jogging around Mediona offers a diverse landscape for runners, characterized by rolling hills, river valleys, and historical sites. The region features a mix of natural trails and paths connecting small villages. Elevation changes are present, ranging from gentle inclines to more challenging ascents. This variety provides options for different fitness levels and preferences. Best jogging routes around Mediona The most popular jogging route is Penedès 360 Trekking: La Cadira Loop, a 6.3 miles (10.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 12 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers scenic views typical of the Penedès region. Another top favourite among local runners is Capellades, La Torre de Claramunt, and Vilanova d'Espoia Loop, a moderate 7.5 miles (12.0 km) path. This running route connects several local towns, providing varied scenery. Local runners also love the Mediona Castle and Les Deus Loop from Sant Joan de Mediona, a 9.0 miles (14.5 km) trail leading through historical areas and natural features, often completed in about 1 hour 54 minutes. Jogging around Mediona is defined by river paths, historical landmarks, and varied terrain including hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy jogging routes Mediona to more challenging running trails. The routes in Mediona are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.8 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 1900 runners have used komoot to explore Mediona's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Mediona?
A: Mediona offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 180 trails to explore. These range from easy paths suitable for beginners to more challenging routes for experienced runners.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails in Mediona?
A: The running trails in Mediona feature a diverse landscape, including rolling hills, scenic river valleys, and paths connecting small villages. You'll encounter a mix of natural trails and varied terrain, from gentle inclines to more challenging ascents, typical of the Penedès region.

Q: Are there easy running routes for beginners in Mediona?
A: Yes, Mediona has several easy running routes perfect for beginners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1131572810/cami-del-riu-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camí del Riu Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a shorter, less strenuous option, ideal for those starting out or looking for a relaxed jog.

Q: What are the typical distances for running trails in Mediona?
A: Running trails in Mediona vary significantly in length. You can find shorter routes around 3 miles (5 km), such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1131572810/cami-del-riu-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camí del Riu Trail&lt;/a&gt;, up to longer distances like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1117203717/mediona-castle-and-les-deus-loop-from-sant-joan-de-mediona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mediona Castle and Les Deus Loop&lt;/a&gt; which is about 9 miles (14.5 km).

Q: Are there circular running routes in Mediona?
A: Many of Mediona's running routes are designed as loops, offering a convenient way to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1132041678/penedes-360-trekking-la-cadira-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penedès 360 Trekking: La Cadira Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1112902180/capellades-la-torre-de-claramunt-and-vilanova-d-espoia-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Capellades, La Torre de Claramunt, and Vilanova d'Espoia Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What can I see along the running routes in Mediona?
A: Mediona's running routes often pass by interesting natural and historical sights. You might encounter natural monuments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2155690" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font d'en Sala&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3028298" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pèlags del Foix&lt;/a&gt;, and some trails even lead past the historic Mediona Castle.

Q: Are there any waterfalls near the running trails in Mediona?
A: Yes, the region around Mediona is known for its natural beauty, including several waterfalls. You can find highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2857736" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Deus Fountains&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3122128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Capellades Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which are often accessible from or near various running paths.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Mediona?
A: The running trails in Mediona are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 3.8 stars from over 40 reviews. Runners often praise the varied scenery, the mix of natural and village paths, and the options for different fitness levels.

Q: Are the running trails in Mediona suitable for families?
A: Many of the easier and moderate running routes in Mediona are suitable for families, especially those with older children. Look for trails with less elevation gain and shorter distances, which offer a pleasant experience for all.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Mediona?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most natural trails in Mediona, especially if kept on a leash. It's always a good idea to check local signage for any specific restrictions, particularly in nature reserves or protected areas, and to ensure you clean up after your pet.

Q: Are there any long-distance running options in Mediona?
A: For those seeking longer runs, Mediona offers several routes that extend beyond 6 miles (10 km). Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1117203717/mediona-castle-and-les-deus-loop-from-sant-joan-de-mediona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mediona Castle and Les Deus Loop&lt;/a&gt; provide a substantial challenge with significant elevation changes, making them ideal for longer training sessions.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Mediona?
A: Spring and autumn are generally considered the best times for running in Mediona, offering mild temperatures and pleasant conditions. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late evening runs are recommended, while winters are typically mild but can be damp.
tips: Beautiful castle that is located above the path that historically connected Sant Joan de Mediona and Sant Quintí, on a cliff and that offers views of the surrounding mountains. You have to take the path that goes up to you directly.

A natural area where you can enjoy lakes, pools, caves and waterfalls. Les Deus are a series of 23 fountains in a row - the water comes from a main source of the Carme Capellades aquifer. In high season, people take advantage of the lakes as a bathing area. On the highest part there is a restaurant and a bar with views and below there is a picnic area. There is also an adventure park where there is via ferrata etc.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Castillo de Mediona y Les Deus circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 14.5 km long. While komoot estimates a jogging time of around 1 hour 54 minutes, the trail is rated as difficult for jogging. For hiking, it's considered moderate, so plan for a longer duration, especially if you want to enjoy the sights like Mediona Castle and Les Deus Fountains.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a varied experience with a mix of natural paths, paved sections, and unpaved tracks. Initially, you'll find flat and straightforward sections, but the route becomes progressively more inclined and exposed. You'll pass through forest areas providing cover, as well as open terrain. The Les Deus area features a canyon with pools and grottoes, where the terrain has been sculpted by water erosion.

faqs.question: Is the Castillo de Mediona y Les Deus trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: For jogging, this trail is rated as difficult due to its length and progressive inclines, making it less suitable for beginner runners. For hiking, it's considered moderate, requiring good physical condition and steady footing. The difficulty increases gradually, so you can assess your fitness as you go.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the historic Mediona Castle, a Romanesque structure dating back to the 10th century, and the natural paradise of Les Deus Fountains, known for its 23 springs, crystal-clear pools, grottoes, and waterfalls. You'll also pass through green fields, vineyards, traditional farmhouses, and hermitages, with panoramic views including Montserrat from certain points.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead in San Joan de Mediona?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in San Joan de Mediona. While specific parking details aren't provided, it's generally advisable to look for public parking areas within the village. As it's a circular route, you'll return to your starting point.

faqs.question: Is the Castillo de Mediona y Les Deus trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the varied scenery with your canine companion. However, always ensure your dog is under control, especially in areas with livestock, other hikers, or near roads. Leash rules may apply in certain sections or protected areas.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but warmer seasons are particularly popular, especially for visiting Les Deus where the lakes are suitable for swimming. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, while summer can be hot, making early morning or late afternoon visits preferable.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the 'Castillo de Mediona y Les Deus — circular desde San Joan de Mediona' trail itself. However, if you plan to visit the Les Deus Aventura adventure park within the Les Deus area, there will be separate fees for their activities.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers significant portions of 'El Camí IT6TR6 El Camí Central: de Castelló de la Plana a Montserrat' (44%), 'Penedès 360 Mountain Bike (ruta 5 dies)' (31%), and 'Penedès 360 Trekking' (26%). You'll also encounter sections of 'Camí de Santa Anna' and 'Camí de les Clivilleres i el Bolet'.

faqs.question: Can I swim in the pools at Les Deus?
faqs.answer: Yes, the lakes and pools at Les Deus Fountains are popular for swimming, especially during warmer seasons. It's a refreshing spot to cool down and enjoy the natural beauty of the grottoes and waterfalls.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail passes through natural landscapes and rural areas. While specific facilities directly on the trail are limited, you will find amenities like cafes and restrooms in San Joan de Mediona at the start and end of your journey. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks for the duration of your activity.
